title: Reactively-reported forms (AE, CM, DV, conditional DD) live on SE_COMMON
description: OpenClinica supports Visit-Based events (scheduled, with start/end dates) and Common events (unscheduled, triggered by event occurrence). Forms reported reactively rather than at scheduled visits belong in a Common event. The default Common event: event_oid=SE_COMMON, event_type=Common, event_title='Common — Reported As Occurring', isRepeating=true. ALWAYS in SE_COMMON: AE (adverse events arise at any time), CM (concomitant medications start/stop independent of visits), DV (protocol deviations occur at any time). CONDITIONALLY in SE_COMMON: DD (Device Deficiency) — only when the protocol requires DD reporting; if no DD requirement, omit the form entirely (do NOT create an empty placeholder). NOT in SE_COMMON: PREGPART (pregnancy testing/reporting follows protocol-designated visits — typically screening + each treatment visit + EOS for FOCBP — so it stays visit-scheduled).
rationale: Scheduling AE/CM at every protocol visit creates artificial empty repeats, confuses sites about when to enter data, and misrepresents the operational model. The Common event type exists in OpenClinica precisely for events that occur at any time during the trial rather than at scheduled visits. Reactively-reported forms placed on SE_COMMON match the OC native pattern and give coordinators one place to log cross-visit occurrences. PREGPART is deliberately excluded because pregnancy testing IS visit-scheduled per protocol — typically at screening, each dosing visit, and EOS for women of child-bearing potential — not reactive.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} pinned to SE_COMMON by §7. Verify event SE_COMMON exists in study.events with event_type='Common', event_title='Common — Reported As Occurring', isRepeating=true. If a DD (Device Deficiency) form is in scope per protocol, also add DD to SE_COMMON. Do NOT add PREGPART — pregnancy testing is visit-scheduled per protocol cadence.

effect.soft: DD form is conditional: include in SE_COMMON ONLY when the protocol explicitly requires Device Deficiency reporting (medical-device studies). If protocol has no DD requirement, omit the DD form entirely rather than creating an empty placeholder. SE_COMMON event creation when absent is study-scope work — the engine currently doesn't have a study-target effect operator for ensuring events exist; handled here in soft until that lands. Customer-library overrides can remove a form from SE_COMMON or move AE/CM to specific visits; surface the deviation in conventions_applied.common_event_applied.forms_excluded_by_override.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §7 during Phase B.1d. SUPERSEDES OC-9 (form_placement.common_visit_safety_admin) from Phase B.1a. §7 is richer: covers AE/CM/DV unconditionally, DD conditionally, names PREGPART as explicitly excluded, defines the SE_COMMON event metadata (event_oid, event_type, event_title, isRepeating). OC-9 covered AE/CM/DV/AESAE flatly without conditional or exclusion logic. OC-9 is being archived in this commit (status set to 'archived' in form_placement.common_visit_safety_admin.json). Note: OC-9 mentioned AESAE; §7 does not. AESAE is the SAE narrative form which is a separate concept from the AE log-line form — under §7 the SAE detail block lives WITHIN the AE form gated on AESER='Y' per OC-7 7F, rather than as its own form. Reviewers using AESAE as a separate form should override at study scope. Set directive emits the visits_assigned pin; SE_COMMON event creation when absent is delegated to soft (study-target effects don't have an 'ensure_event_exists' operator yet).
